raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"A broad request to improve the draft centrally includes strengthening or reframing the thesis, gap, and contribution claims above the sentence level.","R1.2":"Improving the draft requires checking whether the technical argument proceeds coherently from setup to guarantees to conclusions.","R1.3":"The draft makes empirical and theoretical claims, so improvement centrally involves aligning those claims with the reported results and bounds.","R1.5":"A general draft-improvement delegation includes responsibility for section-level narrative order and overall argumentative arc.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because the explicit object is a draft and improvement naturally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and concision.","RX.1":"Any projection of improvement should preserve uncertainty about what can be verified from the artifact alone.","RX.2":"The draft contains strong novelty and deployment claims, so avoiding overclaiming is a central cross-cutting responsibility.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within draft improvement rather than performing unrelated research, implementation, or evaluation work.","RX.4":"Because the artifact concerns evaluator aggregation and production deployment, improvement should avoid language that could encourage unsupported downstream use.","RX.5":"Any proposed responsibility must remain traceable to the provided draft and distinguish artifact-internal checks from external verification."}}

rationale.R1.1: A broad request to improve the draft centrally includes strengthening or reframing the thesis, gap, and contribution claims above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: Improving the draft requires checking whether the technical argument proceeds coherently from setup to guarantees to conclusions.
rationale.R1.3: The draft makes empirical and theoretical claims, so improvement centrally involves aligning those claims with the reported results and bounds.
rationale.R1.5: A general draft-improvement delegation includes responsibility for section-level narrative order and overall argumentative arc.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because the explicit object is a draft and improvement naturally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of improvement should preserve uncertainty about what can be verified from the artifact alone.
rationale.RX.2: The draft contains strong novelty and deployment claims, so avoiding overclaiming is a central cross-cutting responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within draft improvement rather than performing unrelated research, implementation, or evaluation work.
rationale.RX.4: Because the artifact concerns evaluator aggregation and production deployment, improvement should avoid language that could encourage unsupported downstream use.
rationale.RX.5: Any proposed responsibility must remain traceable to the provided draft and distinguish artifact-internal checks from external verification.
